---
title: "Single-case visualization"
output: rmarkdown::html_vignette
vignette: >
  %\VignetteIndexEntry{Visualization — single case}
  %\VignetteEngine{knitr::rmarkdown}
  %\VignetteEncoding{UTF-8}
---

```{r, include = FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(
  collapse = TRUE,
  comment = "#>"
)
```

```{css, echo=FALSE}
pre {
  margin-top: 0.8rem;
  margin-bottom: 1.6rem;
}

.sourceCode {
  margin-bottom: 1.6rem;
}
```
## R code

### 0. Single-case preprocessing & analysis
The visualizations below assume a completed single-case analysis. First run:

```{r, eval=FALSE}
HARMONIC_ANALYSIS(filepath = "../HARMONIC_DIFF/HARMONIC/",
                  type = "standard",
                  full_condition = c("DAY0","DAY4","DAY7","DAY10","DAY14","DAY21"),
                  number_of_rep = c(3, 3, 3, 6, 3, 3),
                  DEG_list_name = "input_DEG_list.txt",
                  mango_design = c("DAY4_DAY0_UP"),
                  core = 1,
                  ref_genome = "mm",
                  PASSED_RATIO = 25,   
                  PASSED_NUM = 3,      
                  similarity = 50,     
                  preprocessing = "F")
```

### 1.1. Tree plot ver. BAR

`HARMONIC_TREE_PLOT_forSINGLE()` summarizes the active trees from a single-case
analysis, showing each tree's HWES and gene distribution ratio alongside its
representative biological process. Key parameters:

- `filepath` — path to the analysis output directory for the comparison of interest
- `trends` — direction of change to plot (`"UP"` or `"DOWN"`)
- `width`, `height` — output figure size (in inches)
- `plot` — plot style (`"bar"` for the bar summary shown below)
- `DEG_list_name` — file name of the input DEG list

```{r, eval=FALSE}
HARMONIC_TREE_PLOT_forSINGLE(filepath = "../HARMONIC_DIFF/HARMONIC/",
                             trends = "UP",
                             width = 8,
                             height = 7,
                             plot = "bar",
                             DEG_list_name = "input_DEG_list.txt")
```

```{r, echo=FALSE, out.width="100%"}
knitr::include_graphics("figures/vis-single-treeplot.png")
```


### 1.2. Tree plot ver. CIR

Setting `plot = "cir"` renders the same active-tree summary in a circular
(polar) layout. Each wedge is one active tree, arranged radially and labeled by
its representative biological process, with tree numbers shown at the center.

```{r, eval=FALSE}
HARMONIC_TREE_PLOT_forSINGLE(filepath = "../HARMONIC_DIFF/HARMONIC/",
                             trends = "UP",
                             width = 8,
                             height = 7,
                             plot = "cir",
                             DEG_list_name = "input_DEG_list.txt")
```

```{r, echo=FALSE, out.width="100%"}
knitr::include_graphics("figures/vis-single-treeplot-cir.png")
```

### 2.1. Term plot ver. BAR

`HARMONIC_TERM_PLOT_forSINGLE()` expands a single active tree into its
individual GO terms, showing each term's Tscore and gene distribution ratio.
Select the tree to expand with `clusternum` (matching the tree number in the
tree plot). Key parameters:

- `filepath` — path to the analysis output directory
- `DEG_list_name` — file name of the input DEG list
- `plot` — plot style (`"bar"` or `"cir"`)
- `trends` — direction of change (`"UP"` or `"DOWN"`)
- `clusternum` — the tree number to expand (matches the tree plot)
- `width`, `height` — output figure size (in inches)
- `LABEL` — title displayed on the plot
- `lfc_leng` — color range for the fold-change scale (applies to the `"cir"` layout)

```{r, eval=FALSE}
HARMONIC_TERM_PLOT_forSINGLE(filepath = "../HARMONIC_DIFF/HARMONIC/",
                             DEG_list_name = "input_DEG_list.txt",
                             plot = "bar",
                             trends = "UP",
                             clusternum = 2,
                             width = 11,
                             height = 5,
                             LABEL = "DAY4 vs DAY0 result")
```

```{r, echo=FALSE, out.width="100%"}
knitr::include_graphics("figures/vis-single-termplot.png")
```

### 2.2. Term plot ver. CIR

Setting `plot = "cir"` renders the term–gene relationships of the selected tree
as a chord diagram: GO terms on one side, their associated genes on the other,
with ribbons linking each gene to the terms it belongs to. This reveals which
genes drive the enrichment of each biological process within the tree.

- `clusternum` — the tree number to expand (matches the tree plot)
- `lfc_leng` — color range for the fold-change scale (used in the `"cir"` layout)

(other parameters as in section 2.1)

```{r, eval=FALSE}
HARMONIC_TERM_PLOT_forSINGLE(filepath = "../HARMONIC_DIFF/HARMONIC/",
                             DEG_list_name = "input_DEG_list.txt",
                             plot = "cir",
                             trends = "UP",
                             clusternum = 2,
                             width = 13,
                             height = 7,
                             LABEL = "DAY4 vs DAY0 result",
                             lfc_leng = 2)
```

```{r, echo=FALSE, out.width="100%"}
knitr::include_graphics("figures/vis-single-termplot-cir.png")
```
